{{nowrap|(from 2 March 2015)}} | captain = [[Nick Slyney]] & [[Richard Mathers]] | stadium = [[The Hive Stadium]] | avg_attendance = | high_attendance = | top_tries = [[Rhys Williams (rugby league)|Rhys Williams]] - 21 | top_goals = [[Wes Naiqama]] - 88 | top_points = Wes Naiqama - 212 | pattern_la1 = _redborder | pattern_b1 = _chevron_redwhitered | pattern_ra1 = _redborder | pattern_sh1 = 000000 | pattern_so1 = _hoops_black | leftarm1 = 000000 | body1 = 000000 | rightarm1 = 000000 | shorts1 = 000000 | socks1 = 000000 | pattern_la2 = _blackborder | pattern_b2 = _chevron_blackwhiteblack | pattern_ra2 = _blackborder | pattern_sh2 = | pattern_so2 = _hoops_white | leftarm2 = DBD7D2 | body2 = AFDBF5 | rightarm2 = DBD7D2 | shorts2 = AFDBF5 | socks2 = 848482 | prev_season = [[2014 London Broncos season|2014]] | next_season = [[2016 London Broncos season|2016]] | list = yes | list_link = List of London Broncos seasons }} The '''2015 London Broncos season''' was the thirty-sixth in the club's history and their first season in the [[RFL Championship]]. Competing in [[2015 RFL Championship|2015 Kingstone Press Championship]], the club was coached by [[Joey Grima]], before being replaced by his assistant [[Andrew Henderson (rugby league)|Andrew Henderson]] in March. They finishing in 7th place, reaching the Championship Shield Final and reaching the Fourth Round of the [[2015 Challenge Cup]]. It was their second since moving to the [[The Hive Stadium|Hive Stadium]]. They exited the Challenge Cup with a defeat by the [[Leigh Centurions]].{{cite web|url=http://nothingbutleague.com/2015/03/24/challenge-cup-match-review-leigh-centurions-64-12-london-broncos/|title=Challenge Cup match review: Leigh Centurions 64-12 London Gains & Losses] ==2015 tables== ===Regular season=== {{:2015 RFL Championship|transcludesection=Regular season|showteam=LON}} ===Championship Shield=== {{Main|2015 Super 8s#Championship Shield}} The third of the three "Super 8" groups saw teams finishing 5th to 12th in the regular Championship table. Like the Super League 8's, these teams retained their original points and played 7 extra games, with the teams finishing in the top 4 places after these extra games contesting playoffs similar to Super League, with 1st v 4th and 2nd vs 3rd, with the winners contesting the Championship Shield Grand Final. The two teams finishing at the bottom of this Super 8s group (7th and 8th) were relegated to the [[League 1 2016 season]], and replaced by the two 2015 League One promoted sides. {{#invoke:sports table|main|style=WDL |res_col_header=QR |winpoints=2 |for_against_style=points |team1=FEA|name_FEA={{leagueicon|Featherstone|16}} [[Featherstone Rovers]] |team2=DEW|name_DEW={{leagueicon|Dewsbury|16}} [[Dewsbury Rams]] |team3=LON|name_LON={{leagueicon|London|16}} [[London Broncos]] |team4=WOR|name_WOR={{leagueicon|Workington|16}} [[Workington Town]] |team5=BAT|name_BAT={{leagueicon|Batley|16}} [[Batley Bulldogs]] |team6=WHI|name_WHI={{leagueicon|Whitehaven|16}} [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] |team7=HUN|name_HUN={{leagueicon|Hunslet|16}} [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] |team8=DON|name_DON={{leagueicon|Doncaster|16}} [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] |win_FEA=19|draw_FEA=0|loss_FEA=11|pf_FEA=809|pa_FEA=701|status_FEA=Q |win_DEW=17|draw_DEW=1|loss_DEW=12|pf_DEW=686|pa_DEW=624|status_DEW=Q |win_LON=15|draw_LON=0|loss_LON=15|pf_LON=756|pa_LON=674|status_LON=Q |win_WOR=11|draw_WOR=1|loss_WOR=18|pf_WOR=587|pa_WOR=782|status_WOR=Q |win_BAT=10|draw_BAT=0|loss_BAT=20|pf_BAT=645|pa_BAT=707 |win_WHI=10|draw_WHI=0|loss_WHI=20|pf_WHI=618|pa_WHI=921 |win_HUN=8|draw_HUN=0|loss_HUN=22|pf_HUN=518|pa_HUN=957|status_HUN=R |win_DON=2|draw_DON=0|loss_DON=28|pf_DON=401|pa_DON=1128|status_DON=R | status_text_Q =Qualified for the Semi Final | status_text_R = Relegated to League 1 |col_Q=#ccffcc|text_Q=Qualified for the
